meta.title: Japan Working Holiday — Insider Living Hacks (Canada → Tokyo)
meta.subtitle: A personal relocation guide. Arrival: NRT, June 30 2026.
meta.status: Research in progress — findings populate automatically as the deep-research workflow completes.

timeSensitive.item: Working Holiday Visa in passport BEFORE you fly
timeSensitive.action: Document prep takes 1–3 months; consulate processing is only ~7–15 business days. The bottleneck is gathering documents. Confirm the visa is stamped before the flight.

timeSensitive.item: Proof of funds (one-way ticket = higher bracket)
timeSensitive.action: Canadian consulates require ~CAD $3,500 WITH a return ticket, or ~CAD $4,500 / +CAD $1,000 WITHOUT one. Your booking is one-way, so budget for the higher figure or add a changeable return.

timeSensitive.item: Address registration at ward office (住民票)
timeSensitive.action: Go to YOUR ward's office (区役所) with passport + residence card + housing contract. This unlocks health insurance, pension, and bank accounts.
Enroll in 国民健康保険 (NHI) and pension. NHI makes medical affordable; pension contributions are partly refundable on departure (lump-sum withdrawal).

timeSensitive.timing: Claim AFTER leaving Japan (within 2 years of departure)
timeSensitive.action: Working-holiday makers who paid pension can claim a partial refund after they leave. Keep your pension book / My Number records.

timeSensitive.item: Re-entry permit before any trip abroad
timeSensitive.timing: Before leaving Japan during your stay
timeSensitive.action: The WHV is effectively single-entry; use the 'special re-entry permit' (tick the box at the airport) before any overseas trip or your residence status lapses.

canadaNotes: Since Dec 1 2024, the Canada–Japan WHV can be granted TWICE in a lifetime (two 1-year stays) or as one continuous 2-year stay.
Eligibility: Canadian citizen, age 18–30, residing in Canada, clean record, good health.
No consular fee — the visa is free.
Proof-of-funds figures vary by consulate (Vancouver, Toronto, Calgary, Montreal) — verify your specific consulate's current page.
You apply at a Japanese consulate IN CANADA before departure; the visa is not issued in Japan.
arrivalSequence: Land at Narita (NRT) → receive your residence card (在留カード) at immigration.
Move into your share house (instant registrable address — no key money or guarantor).
Within 14 days: register your address (住民票) at your ward office.
Same visit: enroll in National Health Insurance + pension.
Open a Japan Post Bank (ゆうちょ) account — the most foreigner-friendly for new arrivals.
Set up phone/eSIM data + bridge spending with Wise + a no-FX-fee Canadian card until local payment is sorted.

top10.tip: Use Wise (multi-currency) from Canada to bridge banking
top10.reason: Opens in minutes, far cheaper FX than banks, covers you until Japan Post is set up.

top10.tip: Open Japan Post Bank first, not a megabank
top10.reason: Only bank that opens for WHV holders without 6-month residency or an employment contract.

top10.tip: Start in a furnished share house
top10.reason: Instant registrable address, no key money/guarantor/2-year lease — unlocks everything else fast.
Post-2023 pricing means individual tickets are often cheaper unless you're doing multiple long Shinkansen legs.

findings.why: Processing is fast (7–15 business days) but document prep is the real bottleneck.
findings.how: Passport, application form + photo, CV, written activity plan, proof of funds bank statement, flight itinerary.

findings.tip: Japan Post Bank opens for new arrivals
findings.why: Most banks demand 6 months residency; Japan Post does not.
findings.how: Bring residence card + registered address (住民票). Visit a post office branch.

findings.tip: Japanese credit card is effectively impossible on a WHV
findings.why: No local credit history; banks decline.
findings.how: Use a no-foreign-transaction-fee Canadian card + Wise debit as the bridge. Rakuten is the eventual long shot.

findings.tip: Share houses skip key money + guarantor
findings.why: A normal lease wants 3–5 months rent upfront plus a guarantor.
findings.how: Oakhouse (largest), Sakura House (newcomer-focused), Borderless House (intl mix), Social Apartments (nicer). Many let you book from abroad.

findings.tip: Start around the Chuo line west or Setagaya
findings.why: Central access, lower cost, social, instant address.
findings.how: Nakano / Koenji / Higashi-Nakano, or Sangenjaya / Shimokitazawa. Kichijoji if you'll pay a bit more for livability.

findings.tip: Data-only eSIM + authenticator app beats a virtual number
findings.why: Most travel eSIMs have no phone number; SMS 2FA is the only thing that breaks.
findings.how: Buy an eSIM (Ubigi/Airalo) ~5 days before flying; move 2FA to an authenticator app or keep CA SMS roaming as backup.
findings.impact: ~$10–20 vs paying for an unused virtual number

findings.tip: Skip tourist IC cards — you're a resident
findings.why: Welcome Suica etc. are for tourists.
findings.how: Add Suica to Apple Wallet or get a personalized commuter pass (定期券) once you know your route.
findings.impact: Commuter pass can save 30%+ on a fixed route

items.task: Confirm WHV eligibility (age 18–30, citizen, residing in Canada)
Gather visa documents: passport, form + photo, CV, written activity plan
items.note: Document prep takes 1–3 months — start early.

items.task: Show proof of funds in your account
items.note: ~CAD $3,500 with a return ticket, ~CAD $4,500 without. Your flight is one-way → budget higher or add a changeable return.

items.task: Book consulate appointment and submit application
items.note: Processing ~7–15 business days. Visa must be in your passport before you fly.

items.task: Check passport validity + blank pages
Open a Wise multi-currency account from Canada
items.note: Bridges banking until Japan Post is set up; cheap FX.

items.task: Move 2FA to an authenticator app (don't rely on SMS abroad)
Reserve a furnished share house (book from abroad if possible)
items.note: Gives you an instant registrable address on arrival.

items.task: Buy a data eSIM (~5 days before flying)
Fill out Visit Japan Web for fast NRT arrival
Get residence card (在留カード) at Narita immigration
Move into the share house
Register address (住民票) at your ward office within 14 days
Enroll in National Health Insurance + pension (same visit)
Open a Japan Post Bank (ゆうちょ) account
Set up Suica in Apple Wallet
First conbini + supermarket run; learn the trash/recycling rules
Slurp a first bowl of ramen in the new neighborhood
Scope coworking spaces + a reliable late-night work café
Lock an async work rhythm for the ~16h Pacific time gap
Find your local sento / public bath
Walk Akihabara end to end
Visit Ochanomizu instrument street + a synth/gear shop
Join one English-friendly dev meetup (check Connpass/Meetup)
Catch a summer matsuri (festival) in yukata
See a big hanabi (fireworks) show
items.note: Sumida River & others run mid–late summer.

items.task: Eat kakigori (shaved ice) on a brutal humid day
Go to Tokyo DisneySea — ride Fantasy Springs
Day trip to escape the heat (Hakone / Kamakura coast)
Tokyo Game Show (~September)
See koyo (autumn leaves) — late Nov in Tokyo parks
Try seasonal foods: sanma, chestnuts, new-crop rice
Catch a sumo basho if one's running
teamLab (Borderless or Planets)
Winter illuminations (start early Dec)
Tokyo Disneyland Christmas season
Hatsumode (first shrine visit) Jan 1
Eat osechi / New Year foods
Mid-year admin check: residence tax, pension on track
Optional: snow trip / onsen weekend
Hanami — cherry blossoms (~late Mar–early Apr)
items.note: Ueno, Meguro River, Shinjuku Gyoen, Chidorigafuchi.

items.task: Hanami picnic with conbini + depachika snacks
AnimeJapan (spring anime convention)
A spring road/rail trip while the weather's perfect
Tick off remaining bucket-list spots & restaurants
Get a re-entry permit if leaving Japan before the visa ends
items.note: WHV is effectively single-entry — tick the box at the airport.

items.task: Close/settle bank account, utilities, phone, lease
File final tax / arrange residence-tax payment
De-register address at ward office before leaving
Claim the pension lump-sum withdrawal refund (within 2 years of leaving)
items.note: Keep pension book + My Number records.

items.task: Last bowl of ramen 🍜
